opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I woke up gasping, and I feel like I am drowning from the inside; I can't breathe unless I sit straight up!
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Woke up suddenly gasping for air about 90 minutes ago and can only breathe sitting bolt upright, now coughing up pink frothy fluid.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden, woke from sleep about 90 minutes ago gasping for air
symptom_narrative.character: severe breathlessness, feeling like drowning, chest tightness
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: extreme — can't breathe at all unless sitting bolt upright
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting bolt upright on the edge of the bed
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: coughing up pink, frothy, blood-tinged sputum over the last 30 minutes, feeling of impending doom, extremely exhausted from the effort of breathing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: known heart failure, missed my water tablet (furosemide) yesterday
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot walk, cannot lie down, sitting bolt upright struggling to breathe

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: spouse at home and very worried; missed his water tablet yesterday and didn't wear his CPAP mask last night

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a consumer pulse oximeter/wearable during severe respiratory distress, may be affected by poor peripheral perfusion

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: terrified, exhausted from the effort of breathing

communication_style.typical_message_style: very short, broken phrases due to severe breathlessness; spouse helping communicate

access_barriers.transport: spouse present, but patient's extreme distress makes self-transport dangerous
